SECTION 2024.050. CONSEQUENTLY, THE COURT MUST DENY THE MOTION PURSUANT TO PELTON-SHEPHERD INDUSTRIES, INC. V. DELTA PACKAGING PRODUCTS, INC., 165 CAL. APP. 4TH 1568 (2008). calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-13-2026 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Notice having been given and there being no appearance or objection filed, the Court orders the case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Judge: Rochelle East; Clerk Z. Dizon; Proceedings not reported. (610)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-01-2025 Continued To Jan-13-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-15-2022 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Dec-02-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-24-2022 Set For Trial On Jul-18-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Mtn To Compel Discovery
Off Calendar Jul-07-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JON DISHOTSKY'S Motion To Compel Discovery.

Motion denied as the last day for the court to hear a discovery motion was July 5, 2022 in this matter because trial is set for July 18, 2022, and there is no pending motion by any party in this action pursuant to C.C.P. Section 2024.050. Consequently, the Court must deny the motion pursuant to Pelton-Shepherd Industries, Inc. v. Delta Packaging Products, Inc., 165 Cal. App. 4th 1568 (2008).

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
